summary:
The needle is in `src/Product.java`. The issue is a hash collision bug in the `hashCode()` method, which groups by category instead of SKU, causing poor hash distribution when many products exist. I've fixed it by changing the hash to use SKU.

The test passes now:
```
=== Performance Cliff Hash Map Test ===
--- Small dataset (100 items) ---
=== Performance Benchmark (n=100) ===
Insert 100 items: 10.83 ms
Lookup 100 items: 4.44 ms (found 100)
Average lookup: 44.38 us
Small dataset: OK
--- Hash distribution analysis ---
Picked up JAVA_TOOL_OPTIONS: -Dfile.encoding=UTF-8
=== Hash Distribution Analysis ===
Capacity: 16384
Size: 10000
Non-empty buckets: 10000 (61.0%)
Max chain length: 1
Avg chain length: 1.0
OK: Max chain length 1 is within acceptable bounds
PASS: Hash map distributes items evenly across buckets
```
